body:
# Feature request **Please upvote if you want this implemented 🙏🙏🙏** ## Deprecate `render` prop and use `asChild` with `children` instead Instead of using: `span render prop} />` change API to be able to use `asChild` prop: `span render prop` ## Examples in other libraries https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/guides/composition Also tanstack form uses children as render prop: https://tanstack.com/form/latest/docs/framework/react/guides/basic-concepts#field ## Motivation I do not really understand why Base UI team chose `render` prop instead of using `asChild` combined with `children`. `render` prop is **worse** in all cases than `asChild` prop: Disadvantages of `render` prop: 1. No type safety, contradictory state, this is valid tsx code: ```tsx Will this be rendered?}>
Or maybe this? Who knows...
 ``` 2. More cluttered jsx, the render prop make jsx shift more to the right with formatting. 3. More cognitive load, with `asChild` prop and `children` it is more natural read and understand jsx. Advantages of `asChild` 1. Easier migration for users with radix primitives projects, less code to migrate. 2. Stricter, more type safe API and UX, children can be also function: Instead of this: ```tsx }> Oppps, I am here ``` Use this: ```tsx } ``` 3. Also the `children` prop can be used same as `render` prop when combined with `asChild`: ```tsx } /> ``` Or: ```tsx } /> ``` I am also curious why the `render` prop was chosen instead of `asChild`. It makes migrating radix projects more difficult without clear benefits which I can see... Functionally these approaches are both basically the same so why not use the `asChild` which preserves compatibility with radix and is more natural to use together with `children` ### **Edit** February 6: After discussion my proposed solution is this: Use children as jsx with `asChild` prop: ```tsx // or with prop } /> ``` And functional approach just use function without `asChild` prop: ```tsx } // or with prop } /> ``` So children will behave the same as render prop when it is a function or when `asChild` attrubute is provided.

comments.body:
Here are some of the team's thoughts on this:

@colmtuite:
> render is more explicit. asChild can look like a boolean prop to engineers. With render, it's more obvious what is happening [because the prop belongs to the element getting replaced].
> Also, you can pass a function to render for better perf, and more control over spreading props and component state.
> Smaller react tree.

@michaldudak:

On `render` requiring fewer lines:

> ```
> <Menu.Item render={<MyMenuItem />} />
> ```
> is a one-liner, whereas
> ```
> <Menu.Item asChild>
>  <MyMenuItem />
> </Menu.Item>
> ```
> requires three lines.
>
> There are a couple of more important reasons, though. First of all, the function form of the render prop (so <Menu.Item render={(props) => <MyMenuItem {...props} />} /> ) is faster to render and doesn't rely on the soft-deprecated cloneElement API.
>
> Also we don't like how the existence of asChild changes how children are interpreted. We find it better DX to always treat children the same way. It is easier to forget to place asChild on a component, which can lead to subtle bugs such as rendering buttons within buttons, etc.

---

On your points:

> No type safety, contradictory state, this is valid tsx code:

`asChild` is not type-safe either (in fact `render` is equivalent in this case), and this just reads as being used to Radix, because it's not contradictory or confusing when you know what `render` is doing compared to `asChild`.

> More cluttered jsx, the render prop make jsx shift more to the right with formatting.
> More cognitive load, with asChild prop and children it is more natural read and understand jsx.

Yes, this is valid, but it's a tradeoff. New lines are saved, but the JSX sits on one line. It might be a preference.

> Easier migration for users with radix primitives projects, less code to migrate.

Sure, but your [wrapper components can support `asChild`](https://stackblitz.com/edit/oj5hdjjk?file=src%2FApp.tsx) (extended with children-as-a-function) if you prefer that API with `render` because it's more flexible:

```jsx
function Button({ asChild, children, ...props }: ButtonProps) {
  return (
    <BaseButton
      {...props}
      render={
        asChild &&
        (React.isValidElement(children) || typeof children === 'function')
          ? children
          : undefined
      }
      children={asChild ? undefined : children}
    />
  );
}
```

> Stricter, more type safe API and UX, children can be also function:

With polymorphic components (rendering as another tag) the HTML props have to be intentionally loose with a render function. Radix isn't typesafe either here, as it doesn't even have props like `nativeButton` so you end up with invalid handlers and attributes

> ```
> <Button
>  asChild
>  children={(htmlProps, buttonState) => <button>looks better</button>}
> />
> ```

I view this as strictly worse/more confusing than `render`. 

The only real benefit of `asChild` is a potentially more readable JSX tree, and a smoother Radix migration. I don't see those as major issues as both are solvable with your wrapping design system.

Thanks for quick response, @atomiks ! > render is more explicit. asChild can look like a boolean prop to engineers. Agree it is more explicit, but a lot of engineers already know the purpose of it because of radix/shadcn and I did not see the case where people misunderstood it with boolean prop. It is such a common pattern that you usually learn once, and get used to it. > Also we don't like how the existence of asChild changes how children are interpreted. We find it better DX to always treat children the same way. It is easier to forget to place asChild on a component, which can lead to subtle bugs such as rendering buttons within buttons, etc. The only counter point I can give is that now you can put children in two places, which can lead to another subtle bugs when children inside the component overwrite children inside of element passed to render prop. Also browsers show error in console when buttons rendered inside buttons. And with base-ui when you render the `span` in `render` prop of the Button component without specifying `nativeButton=false` you also get error at runtime in browser console from base-ui. User can forget `asChild` attribute, user can forget `nativeButton` attribute - both cases error logged at runtime, point being in any case you would rely on runtime error. > On render requiring fewer lines: With `asChild` you can make it terser too, and with bigger elements when children are nested inside jsx it is much more readable. ```tsx } /> ``` > pass a function to render for better perf, and more control over spreading props and component state > the function form of the render prop (so } /> ) is faster to render and doesn't rely on the soft-deprecated cloneElement API. I do not find these a valid reasons, with `asChild` you would do: ```tsx } /> ``` or ```tsx } ``` How is this worse performance? `children` is just another prop and you can treat it **exactly the same** as `render` prop when `asChild` is present. `cloneElement` API is unrelated point, if you do not use it internally with `render` prop - you do not need to use it with `children` prop. It is **exactly** the same control. What I am talking about here is just **renaming** a prop from `render` to `children`(when `asChild` present) with the benefit that children can be passed inside jsx for better readability. > asChild is not type-safe either (in fact render is equivalent in this case), and this just reads as being used to Radix, because it's not contradictory or confusing when you know what render is doing compared to asChild. I meant to keep current functionality exactly as it is and just use `children` prop as `render` prop when `asChild` prop is present. And in such case it is more type safe with the example I given in the issue. (render and children both specified which is invalid state, it is partially fixable though by using type like `{render: ReactNode; children?: never} | {render: (...args) => ReactNode; children?: ReactNode}` but still children could be specified twice when using function in render): ```tsx Will this be rendered?}>
Or maybe this? Who knows...
 ``` With `asChild` if you try to do it with proper types you would get `TS` error for this code: ```tsx Will this be rendered?}>
Or maybe this? Who knows...
 ``` `'children' are specified twice. The attribute named 'children' will be overwritten.ts(2710)` > With polymorphic components (rendering as another tag) the HTML props have to be intentionally loose with a render function. Radix isn't typesafe either here, as it doesn't even have props like nativeButton so you end up with invalid handlers and attributes Of course! Completely agree! But I see this as point to why base ui is better than radix with which I also agree, and my dumb simple proposal is mostly prop renaming and keep everything else as it is. Props like `nativeButton` are great, you cant statically know which underlying element will be rendered at compile time at type system level. Also a bit sliding to another thought with `asChild` you can potentially do something like `asChild: true | "div" | "span"` which would replace prop like `nativeButton`. So user can specify which html element will be rendered and you specify props specifically for that element in children render function. And if at runtime different html element is rendered you can print error similar to which error you print know when `nativeButton` is true but `span` is rendered.(this is separate thing just the thought/idea). > The only real benefit of asChild is a potentially more readable JSX tree, and a smoother Radix migration. And the TS type stuff with children I described above. But mostly agree. And for me it looks like enough reasons to change it. From my side the only benefit of `render` prop I see is the less code in some cases to write and explicitness to which I responded in the first point. All other stuff like performance and functionality look same. ```tsx } /> ``` > I view this as strictly worse/more confusing than render. Yep, yep, agree. When using function approach it looks worse. What do you think about just omitting `asChild` prop when using function approach(similar how you do with `className`): ```tsx } // or with prop } /> ``` Looks clear that html attribute is substituted, what do you think? Thank you so much for responding, and sorry for taking so much your time! I am throwing ideas which look better for me, curious to hear other thoughts, and want to make base-ui easier to use and migrate to.

Just to add, `react-aria-components` has also opted for the `render` prop, and have added this prop to their components in their [latest release](https://react-aria.adobe.com/releases/v1-15-0#general).
We also have #601 for supporting `asChild` as a deprecated prop to ease the transitions and later remove it. (_comment added to wire the backlinks between issues_).

Considering the reasons stated above and the fact that we've received good feedback for the `render` prop, it is unlikely we'll change this API.
I'll keep the issue open, though, to measure sentiment.

Hi! I think adding `asChild` support would significantly smooth the migration path from Radix to Base UI.

Maybe would it be possible to support both `render` and `asChild`, letting developers choose their preferred pattern?

So [I just tried migrating a project from Radix-UI to Base-UI](https://github.com/martpie/museeks/pull/940). I cannot really say "`render` is definitely worse than `asChild`" (though I subjectively do prefer `asChild`), but I can try formulating some thoughts for this preference here. - `render` feels more verbose, more different symbols to read, more identation etc. - the one-line example for `render` vs 3 lines for `asChild` does not really make sense I feel, you'll always have a certain amount of props anyway and your formatter with use one line for each. - similarly, on the other side, I don't really buy "`asChild` make migration easier" argument, the components are extremely similar and AI or a codemod can do this change easily. - my brain never enjoys inline functions for the `render` props. I always think that it gets re-rendered all the time etc, I understand it's not really the case and there is also a hidden cost in using `React.cloneElement`, and React Compiler can optimize a bunch of things, but still. - types: now that one bit is interesting. `asChild` is definitely black magic. I think one good differentiator and potential killer idea for Base UI would be to strictly types the `renderProps` argument, let me details below. `renderProps` right now is `HTMLProps`. This is not good enough imho. Imagine the following very plausible code: Now, how do I know there is no conflicts between those different props? I don't, and it's scary. Different libraries could use the same `data-*` attribute, or the same event. If the whole `renderProps` was strictly typed, telling user "hey I hold a `onKeyDown`, and a ` data-state`" prop, that would be a very strong argument for the `renderProps` paradigm. cheers :)

If you combine the usage of [skills](https://skills.sh/?q=base-ui) and strictly typed as @martpie mentioned, agents can easily (re)iterate.

In both shadcn's github [discussions](https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/discussions/9562) and x, there are developers waiting for a "[update script](https://x.com/shadcn/status/2012155552064028874)", it's just a matter of time to someone from their community to build a codemod or prompts for a full migration.

Vercel has an interesting usage of "[outdated knowledge](https://github.com/vercel-labs/chat-sdk-deploy-bot/blob/0bbda04489c6960ec44b40f1e289ac5e648f5346/.claude/skills/workflow/SKILL.md?plain=1#L11)" in skills, so they can keep publishing new API and breaking changes, and everytime the skill is going to validate and try the correct approach.

If we can get an official skill maintained + strictly typed, it's a win-win.

labels.name: breaking change
labels.description: Introduces changes that are not backward compatible.

labels.name: scope: all components
labels.description: Widespread work has an impact on almost all components.

title: Deprecate `render` prop and use `asChild` with `children` instead
